title: Vanilla Flavored
description: A vanilla-inspired modpack with and emphasis on exploration.
body:
# Vanilla Flavored Vanilla Flavored is a modpack with a whole bunch of mods aimed at improving and adding to the vanilla feel and experience. [Vanilla Flavored 2.0](https://modrinth.com/modpack/vanilla-flavored-2.0) is the 2.0 update. This update brings quite some changes and does deviate a bit from the vanilla feel. This is mainly through the addition of the Ad Astra mod, but using it can be ignored which will keep the vanilla feel a bit higher. ## Installation The modpack can easily be installed via any launcher supporting importing `.mrpack` files or where modpack search is integrated. I personally use the [Prism Launcher](https://prismlauncher.org/). ## Recommended resource packs and shader packs I highly recommend installing [Fresh Animations](https://www.curseforge.com/minecraft/texture-packs/fresh-animations) together with [Fresh Animations Extensions](https://www.curseforge.com/minecraft/texture-packs/fresh-animations-extensions). For shaders I like to use [Complimentary Reimagined](https://modrinth.com/shader/complementary-reimagined), which will have additional setting thanks to the [Euphoria Patches](https://modrinth.com/mod/euphoria-patches) mod. ## Mods in this pack ### Missing mods The following mods should be installed, but due to licencing they must be installed by the end user. Please download them manually and add to the mods folder or add them using your launchers built-in mod manager.
Missing mods
  • SmoothChunk
  • Better Compatibility Checker (CurseForge)
### All mods Here is a list with all mods that are present in the pack. It will be filled out as I have time.
Thanks to all these mods!
  • Falling Leaves (CurseForge)
  • Structory (CurseForge)
  • Effective (CurseForge)

## Server files I'm trying to upload a server-version of the pack where all the client-only mods have been removed. I don't know if this actually improves performance of the server in any way, but my experience is that it works ever-so-slightly better. Though that might just be imagined... Otherwise, you can just run a server using the mods folder and config folder from the pack itself.
